sections.title: Excavation and Trenching Safety Inspection and JHA
sections.html:
PART 1: EXCAVATION AND TRENCHING INSPECTION

Inspection Frequency: Daily prior to start of work, as needed throughout shift, and after every rainstorm or other hazard-increasing occurrence (per 29 CFR 1926.651(k)(1) and WAC 296-155-655(11)).

Inspection Item

Pass

Fail

N/A

sections.title: Excavation and Trenching Safety Inspection and JHA (continued 4)
sections.html:
Step #

Task Step Description

Potential Hazard

Hazard Control Measure

Responsible Party

1

Site Preparation and Utility Locating

Striking underground utilities (gas, electric, water)

Call 811 before digging. Hand dig or pothole to verify utility locations. Maintain safe distance from marked lines.

Competent Person / Operator

2

Excavating / Trenching

Cave-in or soil collapse

Classify soil. Implement protective system (sloping, benching, shoring, or shielding) for trenches >5 ft deep.

Competent Person

3

Spoil Placement

Spoils falling back into trench

Keep spoils and equipment at least 2 feet from the edge of the excavation.

Operator / Laborers

4

Access and Egress

Slips, trips, falls; inability to exit quickly

Provide ladders, ramps, or stairs within 25 feet of travel for trenches >4 ft deep. Ensure ladder extends 3 ft above edge.

Competent Person

5

Working in Trench

Hazardous atmosphere or water accumulation

Test atmosphere if >4 ft deep and potential exists. Inspect for water accumulation daily. Use dewatering equipment if needed.

Competent Person

6

7

8

9

10

911
